title: Traditional vs. Modern Project Management Practices Spectrum
description: A comparative visual diagram illustrating the core distinctions and evolutionary shift from traditional (predictive) to modern (adaptive/agile) software project management methodologies.
elements: Key Differentiating Factors (e.g., Flexibility, Customer Feedback)
relationships: Traditional practices are rigid and sequential, flowing in one direction, while modern practices are iterative and cyclical, embracing change. Hybrid models act as a bridge, incorporating elements of both approaches.
